summary: Comprehensive testing of Phase 1+2 CMS additions (Hero Slides, Feature Highlights, Testimonials, Site Stats) + featured events on home + Buy Tickets wiring. Backend: 19/19 pytest cases passed covering public GET endpoints (active filter + order sort + no _id leak), events?featured=true&limit filter, admin CRUD (POST/GET/PUT/DELETE) for all 4 new collections including persistence verification, inactive items hidden from public, and auth required on admin endpoints. Frontend: Home page renders live data — hero-carousel cycles through 3 DB-backed slides (Celebrating Indian Heritage, Festival of Lights, Programs for Every Generation), hero-stats shows the 4 seeded stats (1967, 1000+, 40+, 58), home-features renders 4 feature tiles, home-upcoming-events shows the 2 seeded featured events (Rising Star, Diwali Mela), home-testimonials shows 3 testimonials. Admin login (admin/admin123) works; sidebar SITE group exposes all 4 new links; navigating to /admin/{hero-slides|feature-highlights|testimonials|site-stats} renders tables with the exact seeded row counts (3/4/3/4). Buy Tickets flow verified end-to-end by creating an event with ticket_types via admin API — the home-buy-tickets-{slug} button appeared on the home card, click opened the TicketPurchaseModal showing the event title, date, and the ticket type ('General $20.00') with quantity +/- controls and a 'Select tickets to continue' CTA.
critical_code_review_comments: models.py HeroSlide uses image_url while initial seed convention in some places of mock.js / past code used 'image' — make sure the admin form (key 'image_url') and any old mock fallbacks stay aligned; tests confirm the live wiring uses image_url end-to-end.

server.py register_admin_crud create() drops keys whose value is None and forwards everything else verbatim to the Pydantic model. Unknown keys are silently accepted-then-dropped by Pydantic's default (extra='ignore') — typos in admin payloads will not surface; consider model_config = {'extra':'forbid'} for the new CMS models to catch typos early.

GET /api/events?featured=true&limit=N applies limit AFTER sort by created_at desc; this means newest featured events win regardless of an 'order' field. If event ordering on home is later required, expose an 'order' or 'priority' field on Event and sort by it.

Public list endpoints (/hero-slides, /feature-highlights, /testimonials, /site-stats) return ALL fields including timestamps. That's fine, but consider trimming 'created_at' from public payload for slightly smaller responses.

Buy Tickets button visibility condition in Home.jsx (ticket_types present AND registration_open) matches expectation; verified by creating a test event with ticket_types — button appeared and modal opened correctly. No issues found.
AdminLayout sidebar links lack data-testid attributes — adding them would simplify future automated testing of admin navigation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all flows verified — hero carousel, stats, features, upcoming events, testimonials, admin sidebar + 4 admin pages, Buy Tickets modal)

test_credentials: Admin: admin / admin123. Backend tests create/delete TEST_* records for hero-slides, feature-highlights, testimonials, site-stats and events; one transient test event ('test-buy-tickets-evt') was created for the Buy Tickets UI verification and deleted at the end.
seed_data_creation: Verified pre-existing seed: 3 hero_slides, 4 feature_highlights, 3 testimonials, 4 site_stats (matches main agent's verification). No persistent test data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test suite for the CMS additions is at /app/backend/tests/test_home_cms.py. Re-run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_home_cms.py -v --tb=short. The earlier suite test_members_and_admin.py also still passes. New collection admin payload shapes (matching backend Pydantic models): HeroSlide requires image_url (NOT 'image'); Testimonial requires name + body (NOT 'author'/'quote'); FeatureHighlight requires title + description; SiteStat requires label + value. The Buy Tickets button data-testid is home-buy-tickets-{slug} (slug-based), not the event id. The admin login inputs use placeholder='Username'/'Password' (no data-testid) — main agent could add testids for easier future automation.
rca of the issue: No production issues found. During initial test authoring, three of my POST tests failed (500 Internal Server Error) because I used incorrect field names ('image', 'quote'/'author'). Backend logs showed Pydantic ValidationError missing 'image_url'/'body'. Updated tests to match the real model schemas (HeroSlide.image_url, Testimonial.name+body) and all 19 tests passed. The frontend AdminPages already uses the correct field keys, so this was purely a test-author error and NOT a product bug.
